Although I stayed at The Apurva Kempinski Bali two years ago, I feel compelled to write this review now, simply because I regret not doing so at the time. I completely forgot to leave feedback back then, yet this hotel left such a profound mark on me that it still comes to mind frequently. Even after two years, the image of this place remains vivid, which alone speaks to the depth of the experience it offers. From the very beginning, the hotel impresses with a sense of scale and presence that is hard to put into words. The architecture is spectacular: powerful, symbolic, and deeply connected to Balinese heritage while seamlessly expressing modern luxury. Arriving there feels almost ritualistic. This is not merely a hotel, it is a landmark and an experience in itself. What truly elevates this place is the human element. The service is delivered with warmth, grace, and remarkable professionalism. You never feel anonymous or rushed. Instead, there is a genuine sense of being cared for in a thoughtful and natural way. Every interaction carries a feeling of sincerity rather than formality, which creates an atmosphere of ease and trust. The accommodation is exquisite, generous in space, beautifully composed, and designed to reflect both elegance and cultural identity. Everything is pristine and carefully maintained, offering a sense of privacy and serenity that makes the room feel like a personal retreat rather than just a place to sleep. Dining at the resort is an experience of its own. The culinary standard is consistently high, and breakfast stands out as a daily highlight: rich in choice, refined in presentation, and impressive in quality. The food never feels routine, instead, it feels like an integral part of the journey, adding another layer to the overall impression of the resort. The resort itself is expansive and filled with natural beauty. Moving through the grounds feels like wandering through a curated tropical world: from the pools to the beachfront to the shared spaces, everything flows in harmony with the surroundings. There is a strong sense of calm and balance throughout the property, as if the environment itself invites you to slow down. The spa experience was exceptional, and the massages were absolutely PERFECT, deeply relaxing, highly professional, and delivered with outstanding skill. What makes this hotel truly special is not only its beauty or service, but the way it stays with you long after you leave. It is one of those rare places that imprints itself on your memory. Even now, two years later, it remains clearly distinct from many other luxury hotels I have visited. I sincerely regret not writing this review earlier, because this hotel deserves recognition of the highest kind. It left such a strong emotional impression on me that I still remember the feeling of being there, and that, to me, is the ultimate sign of excellence. This is not just somewhere you stay. It is somewhere you remember❤️

The Apurva Kempinski Bali is nothing short of a man-made heaven, a place where architecture, luxury, and nature come together at an extraordinary scale. The moment you see it, you realize this is not just a hotel — it’s a massive landmark resort designed to impress from every angle.

The structure itself is breathtaking. Built like a grand open-air palace, the hotel cascades down towards the ocean, offering next-level views and vibes from almost every corner. The design reflects Indonesian heritage blended with modern luxury, making it one of the most visually stunning resorts in Bali. Every hallway, lobby, and viewpoint feels like a curated masterpiece.

The resort is huge, with multiple restaurants, lounges, pools, beach access, and activity spaces spread across the property. Each restaurant offers a distinct culinary experience, ranging from fine dining to relaxed beachfront meals, all maintained at world-class standards. Despite its size, everything is extremely well organized and thoughtfully planned.

The Apurva Kempinski is ideal for long stays. With spacious rooms, villas, wellness facilities, and peaceful surroundings, it’s easy to spend days here without feeling the need to step out. There’s always something to explore within the property itself. No surprise that the hotel is almost always sold out, especially during peak seasons.

What truly sets this resort apart is the energy and atmosphere. Sunrise, sunset, and night-time views are magical, creating moments that feel cinematic and unforgettable. It’s rare to see architecture and luxury executed at this scale with such attention to detail.

Overall, The Apurva Kempinski Bali is not just a stay — it’s an experience. A destination in itself, and easily one of the most beautiful luxury resorts ever created. 🌴✨

We woke up early one morning and went to lobby to ask about access and were told by Mr Trisna that it was “not possible” until the gym opened at 6:00 AM.  We specifically asked if we could just walk on the treadmill, but he again repeated “not possible”.  Disappointed, we decided to wait for over an hour.  We then saw an obvious gym patron walking through the lobby at 4:45 AM so went to gym and it was already open before 5:00 AM, just as we suspected.  Already 5 or 6 folks working out.

So this desk manager, Mr Trisna, without having any actual knowledge or training, gave us wrong information and created unnecessary stress.  Why?
Please train customer-facing employees with correct information so they can serve customers instead of tainting an otherwise positive guest experience.

A Pinnacle of Luxury and Hospitality at Cliff Lounge

​Rating: ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

​Our stay at The Apurva Kempinski Bali was made truly unforgettable by our daily visits to the Cliff Lounge. If you are staying at the resort, the Cliff Lounge experience is an absolute must.

​Culinary Excellence: Breakfast & Afternoon Tea
​The breakfast buffet is, quite simply, world-class. The sheer variety and quality of the selection are staggering:

​Global Flavors: We were blown away by the extensive spread of Western, Asian, and Indonesian cuisines. Every dish tasted authentic and was prepared with high-quality ingredients.

​Personalized Touches: As fans of traditional flavors, we were thrilled that the team was able to prepare fresh Indian Chai for us every single morning—it became the highlight of our start to the day.

​Afternoon Tea: The transition to afternoon tea was seamless, offering a sophisticated selection of treats and a serene atmosphere to unwind.

​Service That Goes Above and Beyond
​What truly sets the Cliff Lounge apart is the staff. They don't just provide service; they provide genuine care.
​Aditya: A special shout-out to Aditya, who was phenomenal. He consistently went out of his way to ensure we had everything we needed before we even had to ask. His proactive attitude and warmth made us feel like VIPs.

​The Culinary Team: We were so impressed by the Chef, who made a point to come out every morning to speak with us personally. He genuinely cared if our food met our expectations and was eager to customize orders to our liking.

​Atmosphere & Ambiance
​The lounge offers a breathtaking panoramic view of the Indian Ocean, providing a much quieter and more exclusive feel compared to the main dining areas. The infinity pool attached to the lounge is the perfect spot for a quiet dip between coffee and snacks.
​Final Verdict: The team at the Cliff Lounge operates like a well-oiled machine, but with a deeply personal heart. From the incredible buffet selection to the attentive care from Aditya and the kitchen staff, they exceeded every expectation. We cannot recommend this experience enough

Just returned from a 5-night stay at the Kempinski hotel, Budapest. The hotel is excellent … the staff were first-class and attended to all our needs … and the facilities were top notch … especially the spa and pool area which we used a lot. Finally, the location is very central and perfect for exploring Budapest. I would highly recommend this hotel for couples and families alike.

Architecturally, this is the most impressive resort in Bali. It is a massive, modern-day temple built into a cliffside. I spent the New Year here, and the resort handled the festivities and special menus with a level of "pampering" and scale that was truly memorable.

Room Strategy: Your experience here depends heavily on your room category. I stayed in an Ocean Front Suite near the Beach Club. While the room was beautiful, the club’s music and energy can be loud throughout the day. In retrospect, I would recommend the Cliff Private Pool Suites. These are the unique architectural feature of the resort, offering spectacular, quiet views over the Indian Ocean from much higher up the cliff.

World-Class Dining: The food quality here is top-tier on a global level.

- Izakaya by OKU: Outstanding Japanese cuisine. The four-course tasting menu is a standout, and even the ramen is high-end.

- Pala: One of the best breakfast buffets I have ever experienced. The variety is massive, though it is popular and can get very busy during peak hours.

- Koral: The aquarium dining is a must-experience for the atmosphere, especially for romantic dinners.

- Bai Yun: This Chinese restaurant is prestigious enough to host diplomats. I spoke with the chef about his experience preparing state dinners, and that level of precision is evident in the food.

Wellness & Facilities: The resort is a "mega-resort" in every sense. The scale is breathtaking, but it means a lot of walking or waiting for buggies. The service remains responsive despite the size of the property.

Service: Exceptionally quick and responsive. The "Lady in Red" took incredible care of me throughout the stay. When I realized I had left a small item behind after moving rooms, they personally searched the room and recovered it for me. I was pleasantly surprised by that level of proactive care in a resort of this size.

Verdict: Go for the grand architecture, the Cliff Suites, and the incredible food. If you prefer a quiet, intimate, and boutique feel, consider the Ritz-Carlton next door. But for grandeur and festive energy, the Kempinski is the definitive choice.

I've travelled extensively for work and been a tour leader for many years working in travel for 18 in total. I've never been so impressed with a hotel when I walked in as I was with the Kempinski! So this time I decided to stay there. 

The room was lovely and spacious (Grand deluxe ocean court room twin), the service was excellent. The grounds are really well kept, its not like other Indonesian hotels I've stayed at where its nice but theres little things falling apart everywhere - nope everything is perfect.

The breakfast buffet is massive with so much to choose from. I loved the pool which has a view out to the ocean.

The afternoon tea (high tea) was amazing - such good value and one of the best I've ever done.

The only small negatives I have are, the staff while amazing, had memorised that I had a cinnamon allergy and took pleaseure in "showing this off" every morning at breakfast and every time I went to a restaurant. However, while they knew I had this allergy, the made no effort to actually inform me which items contained cinnamon. This was not great at breakfast as the french toast and waffles both contained a large amount of cinnamon. It was only those two items, it would have been great if they'd said along with "ma'am we know about your cinnamon allergy" - "the waffles and French toast contain cinnamon". 

The other was the beds in the rooms, while spacious for a twin, are quite hard I feel like they may be catering to the Chinese market quite a bit. Having worked in China extensively, I know very well they like a hard bed!

Other than those two small things, our stay was perfect, it was a nice little treat to start our holiday and I will most certainly be back!

A Hotel of Superlatives

During our trip to Indonesia, we spent a week in Jakarta, followed by a week in Ubud, and finally at the Kempinski Hotel in Bali. This stay was undoubtedly the highlight of our trip.

We regularly travel to various Kempinski hotels for both business and pleasure, and therefore have very clear expectations of this establishment. These expectations were met in every respect in Bali – and in many ways even exceeded.

The hotel impresses on all levels with its exceptional professionalism and service culture. All the staff are extremely friendly, always ready to help, and always greet guests with a genuine smile. This attitude is consistently reflected throughout the entire hotel, creating an atmosphere where you feel welcome and perfectly at ease from the very first moment.

We had a suite with a garden view, a private pool, and a private balcony. The furnishings were superb, of the highest quality, and tastefully designed. Everything was impeccably clean, very well-maintained, and overall at an exceptionally luxurious level.

The resort itself is impressively large, very spacious, and at the same time superbly organized. The beach is very clean, tidy, and quiet, making it a wonderful place to relax. The lobby is also architecturally impressive and immediately conveys the feeling of a top-class hotel.

The hotel's culinary offerings are equally outstanding. All the restaurants are excellent, both in terms of quality and atmosphere. The "Koral" restaurant, with its enormous aquarium, deserves special mention – the food there was not only delicious but also visually stunning, and the entire experience was simply breathtaking. The other restaurants in the hotel are equally impressive.

This stay was a true highlight for us. We will definitely return and can wholeheartedly recommend the Kempinski Hotel in Bali without reservation.
The place is beautiful.Its one of the things that money can buy🥰🥰🥰
amazing😍

Loved the rooftop bar and canapes (go right on opening to get a front table), good breakfast and great Japanese restaurant. The included spa day was lovely.
BUT... ........
The pool was slimy, slippery underfoot and had algae all over. There is no pool bar as advertised, just a little hut to order drinks. The Reef Beach club was grubby, hot and very poor, certainly not worth paying for.  The adjoining  all day cafe was painfully slow. Over one hour for food each time. Even though right on the sand it is  disappointing as it is practically enclosed with very little view.
The kids slide was rusty and mouldy in disrepair.
Overall very disapointing from an exceptional building, there was no atmosphere what so ever. No Balinese experience evident activities and apart from the restaurants no guest experience at all.
It is isolated from the rest of the peninsular and effort  for the guests enjoyment is totally lacking. A very bland hotel.

My recent stay at The Apurva Kempinski Bali was an exceptionally luxurious experience that exceeded expectations in numerous aspects. This five-star beachfront resort offers an unparalleled combination of elegance, comfort, and stunning natural beauty. While the property demonstrates remarkable strengths in service quality and amenities, there are certain areas that warrant attention to enhance the overall guest experience.

This resort is particularly well-suited for honeymoon couples and romantic getaways, given its intimate atmosphere, stunning views, and luxurious amenities. The combination of privacy, service excellence, and natural beauty creates an ideal setting for special occasions.

For families or business travelers, the remote room locations and occasional maintenance issues may present minor inconveniences. However, these are outweighed by the exceptional service quality and overall luxury experience.

The Apurva Kempinski Bali stands as a premier luxury destination that delivers an extraordinary tropical resort experience. With minor improvements in maintenance and climate management, this property could achieve unparalleled excellence in the competitive luxury hospitality market. Despite these small shortcomings, the resort remains highly recommended for discerning travelers seeking sophistication, comfort, and unforgettable beachfront luxury in Bali.

What truly elevated the experience was the impeccable level of service. The staff were consistently attentive, intuitive, and genuinely thoughtful. A particularly memorable moment was when a staff member noticed me approaching the elevator from a distance and proactively pressed the button, allowing the doors to open precisely as I arrived — a subtle yet powerful reflection of Kempinski’s service excellence.

After settling into the room, the team thoughtfully checked if I required additional water or towels. When the bidet remote control briefly malfunctioned, the issue was addressed immediately and professionally.

Breakfast offered an extensive and well-curated selection, with restaurant staff demonstrating excellent attentiveness and efficiency. As a gracious farewell, I was presented with a passport holder amenity, a refined and thoughtful closing touch to the stay.

As part of our Indonesia trip, we stayed in Jakarta for a week, then in Ubud for a week and finally at the Kempinski Hotel in Bali. This stay was clearly the crowning conclusion of the trip for us.

We regularly travel to various Kempinski hotels, both privately and professionally, and have correspondingly clear expectations of this house. These expectations were met in Bali in every respect – and even exceeded in many respects.

The hotel impresses on all levels with its exceptional professionalism and service culture. The entire staff is extremely friendly, always helpful and always treats guests with an honest smile. This attitude runs consistently through all areas of the house and creates an atmosphere in which you feel welcome and in good hands from the first moment.

We had a suite with garden view, private pool and private balcony. The equipment was excellent, extremely high quality and at the same time tastefully designed. Everything was spotlessly clean, very well maintained and overall on a decidedly luxurious level.

Before my visit, I had heard about the chapel near the beach and was interested in seeing it. Upon check-in, I asked the receptionist if it would be possible to view the chapel from the outside. I made it clear that I didn’t need to enter, just to take a look at the interior from a distance. The receptionist assured me that the chapel was open to hotel guests and that I was welcome to take a look, especially if the door was open.

Excited, I went to the chapel and found the door open. I was simply peeking inside when a security guard approached in a rather rude manner, saying, 'You cannot enter, this is not a public place.' I tried to explain that I was a hotel guest, showed my room key, and mentioned that the receptionist had informed me I could view the chapel. I hadn't even stepped inside; I was just looking from the doorway.

I was left feeling disrespected and frustrated by the security guard's attitude. Despite this unpleasant experience, the rest of my stay was quite enjoyable, but I feel this interaction could have been handled more professionally.

We really enjoyed our stay at The Apurva Kempinski Bali. Every little detail was simply perfect! Our spacious and beautiful villa with a private outdoor lounge area and swimming pool offered exceptional comfort.
The overall hotel design is absolutely outstanding - you can’t help but say WOW the moment you see it. The hotel grounds are very well maintained, full of greenery and charming Christmas decorations that made the atmosphere even more special.
We visited most of the hotel’s restaurants and enjoyed all of them, but the Villa Lounge with its excellent breakfasts was simply the best (thank you, Doni, and the entire team!). The New Year’s Eve Gala Dinner in the main lobby was simply unforgettable.
The hotel also has a fantastic gym - very spacious and clean, and fully equipped with everything one could wish for.
And the hotel staff… they did an incredible job making us feel welcomed and cared for. Special thanks to Amar from the Villa Management team, who ensured that every our little request was handled perfectly.

I would definitely recommend this hotel to everyone traveling to Bali and would absolutely love to return again.

As a frequent Kempinski guest and Titanium member, I was genuinely shocked by our experience at the Kempinski Hotel Corvinus Budapest.
We originally booked four nights (14-18 December) but  to cancel and leave midway through our stay due to the consistently very poor experience. The breakfast, in particular, was far below Kempinski standards. It was disorganized, overcrowded and confusing. Guests were queuing for eggs while others could supposedly order à la carte — if you were lucky enough to get served. Basic items such as avocado toast or Eggs Benedict required an extra charge, which is completely unacceptable for a five-star Kempinski hotel.

The food quality and variety were disappointing and did not reflect the brand at all. I find it hard to believe that many of the five-star reviews come from guests familiar with Kempinski properties; several other negative reviews accurately reflect our experience.

Upon arrival, there was no welcome amenity in the room, which is standard at Kempinski hotels — especially for Platinum or Titanium members. Only after raising a complaint was something provided. In the room, there was only body wash provided — as already mentioned in other negative reviews — yet clearly nothing has changed. Ironically, the spa does offer proper hair shampoo, which only further highlights the inconsistency in standards.

The lobby atmosphere was another major disappointment. It feels like a busy passageway rather than an elegant hotel lobby, with constant foot traffic and no sense of exclusivity. Tables from the EZ Bistro in this passage were frequently left uncleared, giving the area the appearance of a food court in a shopping center rather than a luxury hotel.

Due to these issues, we requested the cancellation of our remaining two nights and had to book another hotel on short notice, which was both stressful and costly. While we ultimately found a proper five-star hotel elsewhere, this significantly impacted our vacation.

On top of the cancellation and apology from the Lady in Red, we received a chocolate box that was already past its expiration date! Sadly, this says a lot about the overall level of attention to detail and the quality of service/food.

I regret not requesting reimbursement for the nights we stayed, but at that point, we simply wanted to leave. I will be escalating this experience to Kempinski management, as this property seriously risks damaging the reputation of the brand.

The private pool exceeded our expectations—it was significantly larger than what we had seen in the photos and became the perfect place to unwind after long days of exploring. The Japanese restaurant is nothing short of legendary and a true highlight of the stay.

As Discovery Titanium members, we were sincerely impressed by the recognition and generosity shown to us. Receiving a double upgrade during peak occupancy is rare, and we were genuinely grateful for the thoughtful gesture.

A few areas offer room for refinement. Communication with room service staff could be smoother, pricing would benefit from clearer logic and consistency, and food delivery times were longer than expected, with one order taking close to 1.5 hours.

Overall, this is an outstanding hotel that delivers a memorable experience. We truly enjoyed our stay and look forward to returning again without hesitation 🙏❤️

Great location and very comfortable stay (3 nights in December)
The hotel is perfectly located in the heart of Budapest within walking distance of everything. The room was clean, quiet and the bed was comfortable. Not the best room view we got but still highly recommended.

We had a fantastic stay and were truly impressed with this property. The service was impeccable — warm, attentive, and genuinely personal. The staff at the kids’ pool were outstanding, always attentive and even knowing our children by name, which made us feel completely at ease. The kids’ pool with slides was a big hit and a real highlight for our family.

The property itself is beautiful, with excellent facilities throughout. Our room was clean, spacious, and very comfortable. Breakfast at the lounge level was very good, with a great selection and relaxed atmosphere.

The kids’ club was good and had some nice activities with the highlight being the visit of the Koral Aquarium on the property, though the indoor space could benefit from an upgrade (e.g. use the space better with a jungle gym, slides on one side). The outdoor playground has just been extended, which is great to see, but additional sun cover would make it even better.

Overall, an amazing property with thoughtful service and great family offerings. The beach was beautiful as well. We had a wonderful time and will definitely be returning with our family!

Our room is big, glad it has bathtub and separate toilet and bath. Perfect for my family of 3!

Breakfast spread is good as well and stations are well separated so crowding is avoided -- of course in exemption of the cook-to-order egg station as there's only 1 preparing so the queue can get a little bit long especially during peak hours.

I found this hotel on YouTube during the COVID-19 pandemic and finally made it to my visit.

[Overall]
I was extremely satisfied with this hotel. If I ever visit Bali again, I'll definitely return. The lobby had a spectacular atmosphere. It was worth the trip just to see it.

[Front Desk, Service]
We arrived at the hotel after 10 PM due to an overnight flight. I'd contacted them in advance to let them know I'd be late, and check-in was smooth. The staff were simple and unobtrusive. They were a little close, but not too shy.

[Room]
We stayed in the Grand Deluxe Lagoon. The overall design was simple yet relaxing. The lighting was a little dim overall. My wife and I had a few large suitcases, so we only had one space to unpack, which was a bit of a challenge. The water supply was limited, but we requested more, and housekeeping provided more the next day.

The shower and toilet were easy to use, with a typical Japanese feel. While it may have just been our room, we were bothered by noise on the first day, including footsteps from upstairs and children from the next room, so we had to consult the front desk the next day. We had hoped for a room change, but the hotel couldn't accommodate, and offered to change rooms. We liked our room, so we declined, but the noise was a bit of a problem.

[Meals]
The breakfast was excellent. Everything was delicious. I had bread, egg dishes, Balinese food, porridge, noodles, yogurt, etc.

I ate in the same place every morning, and the staff remembered my coffee and other orders and made smooth suggestions. Since I often overate at breakfast, I opted for a light lunch at the in-room dining area. It may be pricey by local Bali standards, but it's about the same price range as a regular meal in Japan. The meal was delicious and not particularly unappealing. In the evening, we went to the rooftop bar and Baiyun. Each had its own unique charm and was enjoyable.

[View, Pool]
When considering this hotel, the view of the palace-like hotel exterior from the pool was a must-see. The main pool was very large, so even when there were many people in the pool, it didn't bother me. I was able to relax by the poolside both mornings. By the way, when I walked over to the Ritz-Carlton next door, I saw that the pool was small, so I decided to choose the Kempinski. Relaxing in the pool while looking at the hotel from inside is the perfect holiday. It's also impressive to see the hotel gradually light up from the rooftop bar during the magic hour at dusk. The good seats fill up quickly, so I recommend going right after opening to get a good seat. If you go later, there won't be any seats and it will be difficult to get in.

Personally, it was a great hotel that I would like to return to.

The Apurva Kempinski Bali is a visually arresting, ultra-luxury resort that feels more like a monumental destination than a hotel. The architecture is grand and dramatic, with sweeping views, cavernous spaces, and a strong sense of place rooted in Indonesian design.
Service is polished and attentive, rooms and villas are expansive, and the beach is among the best in Nusa Dua. Dining is solid—some outlets shine more than others—but overall quality remains high. The scale can feel overwhelming at times, yet it delivers a true “wow factor” rarely matched in Bali.
Bottom line: ideal for travelers seeking grandeur, space, and a statement luxury stay rather than boutique intimacy.

I’ve stayed at many decent hotels and resorts, but Kempinski Nusa Dua is definitely a place you’d want to come back to.

-Villa
I stayed in a villa, which I highly recommend if you value privacy. Villa guests have access to a private lounge that’s available almost all day — breakfast(12pm), afternoon tea(5pm), and drinks in the evening. It really adds to the overall experience.

The facilities are excellent and still feel very new. All minibar items are complimentary, which was a nice touch.

What truly stood out was the staff. Every single person was attentive, warm, and genuinely caring — they are one of the biggest reasons I wanted to extend my stay.

One thing to note: some villas don’t get much direct sunlight. If that matters to you, I’d recommend requesting a unit with good sun exposure over the pool area.

If privacy is your priority, go for a villa. If you prefer a more open vibe and ocean views, the beach-facing suites are a better choice.

-Beach & Pool
The beach has just the right amount of waves — great for light water sports or simply chilling in the water. There are three large main pools, so it never feels crowded.

-Food
I’m quite easy-going when it comes to food, and overall the dining here is solid. Any restaurant you try on the property will be reasonably good and satisfying.

-Spa & Massage
The spa facilities are spacious and well-designed, but availability is quite limited. I only managed to book a foot massage for once, which was unfortunately underwhelming — it was just basic rubbing rather than a proper treatment.

But prices are relatively affordable compared to other nearby five-star resorts, especially considering the quality of service and facilities.

Overall, I had an amazing stay and would definitely come back here for another vacation.

My husband and I's experience staying at the Apurva Kempinski exceeded our expectations. From the moment we arrived, the entire staff was very helpful, and the check-in process was quick and easy. We booked a deluxe ocean view room and were upgraded to a cliff suite with a private pool at no additional cost. Many thanks to Mr. Rendi (front liner), who was very helpful during our stay at the Apurva Kempinski. He was very polite, friendly, and responsive in serving us. If we ever visit Bali again, we will definitely stay at the Apurva Kempinski again.

I went to the Pala Restaurant. Indonesian Indian cuisine. Worth the experience for romantic dinner, got extra mocktail for honeymoon couples (Address your occasion during reservation). Free buggy ride to the restaurant.  Amazing view, like the Infinity Castle from Demon Slayer

The hotel fully matches the description and proud Name of Kempinski , excellent territory ,pools , service , especially pleased Restaurant Coral , if you want to get there better book in advance . Special thanks to the Russian-speaking hotel employee Ratna, always in touch, answers any questions.
The waitress at the Blue Fox wanted to pour champagne on a half-filled glass...

What an incredible hotel!   It's perfect in every way.

All staff are outstanding but we do have to single out Sujite who serve us at breakfast each morning.  He was very considerate and attentive.   There was a different noodle dish each morning and they were all so good!!

All of the restaurants are outstanding.  Even if you are not staying at the Kempinski you must get a reservation for dinner at Koral.  Wow!!
We went as a family and the location was amazing. The food was excellent, and the breakfast was absolutely outstanding. Danu and Cikal from the restaurant were incredibly helpful with the kids.
Excellent location and breakfast…

A bit of a challenging start of my stay, but then things worked out quite well.

Stayed for 4 nights in October as part of the delegation of one of the largest global banks attending a finance conference in Budapest. Because I was arriving on Tuesday at 8am at the  hotel, I booked a room from Monday onwards, to be sure that at my arrival I had my room read without the need to wait until 3PM, the usual check in time. On my reservation it was clearly stated "later arrival".  In fact, on the day of my arrival, very early in the morning, I emailed the hotel to confirm that I was on my way, and also asking to have an ironing board in my room at my arrival.   So...the unpleasant surprise was that when I arrived I was told that I needed to wait because my room was not ready, what I didn't accept and had to push very hard, explaining the reception staff that my room should have been ready since the day before, and that because it was fully paid and guaranteed, they should have not given it to someone else. I had to raise the tone of my voice and be very clear on my frustration for suddenly an available room to "appear"....not good. Not at all.  I wasn't asking for a favour but for what I paid.  Btw...ironing board not in the room, so had to call a few times and ask for it.

Leaving this aside, stay was very good. Hotel is very well located, nice room, clean, well equipped, with nice amenities, good functioning wifi, friendly staff, nice bar for a casual drink in the evening.

I can confidently say that The Apurva Kempinski is the most beautiful beach hotel I have ever seen. The scale of the property is breathtaking—from the impressive, almost overwhelming lobby to the meticulously manicured gardens and stunning decor. The rooms are fantastic and perfectly complement the grandeur of the hotel.

Dining here was a highlight of the trip. Both the Japanese and Chinese restaurants were absolutely out of this world—a must-try for any guest.

regarding service, the management team was very attentive to our special requests. Because of the sheer size of the resort, service can sometimes feel a little slow, but the employees more than make up for it. The staff is super nice, always smiling, and genuinely willing to please the guests. A truly magnificent experience.
Hotel in a strategic position, excellent room and efficient service, also recommended with children, we will certainly return

Bayonne's Ariana and Nancy were kind.
It ate too well and came out to fill.
Thank you and I thought I would like to come back to Campinski next time! Thank you!

I had an amazing experience at Apurva Kempinski! This hotel is everything! A special thanks to the lady in red, Stefany at the front desk and the entire staff.  I definitely will visit again! Expectational service on all levels!

This is the most magnificent 5-star hotel, featuring the finest rubies. You can enjoy a panoramic view of the blue sea and the facilities, and relax in nature with a private pool that feels like being in a jungle. The rooms are spacious and are cleaned daily. Throughout the hotel, you can admire sculptures depicting the unique Hindu culture of Bali, Indonesia.

The breakfast is diverse, and the egg dishes and local cuisine are particularly delicious. You can also enjoy free exercise activities at the hotel, such as yoga and canoeing. I would definitely like to return.

Stayed here using the AMEX fine hotels credit. We had 3 rooms and everything worked out great. The breakfast here is amazing and we had a great view out our window. We love Budapest’s Christmas markets and this is in an excellent location to enjoy them all. The hotel was very busy and that’s the only issue we had. Other than that it was a great stay with a very helpful concierge for getting tickets to the various Christmas events.
Indra and Lydia

The hotel had an excellent location with cheap taxis available, and it was spotless in every area. 
The staff were amazing, especially the lady in red whose service was exceptional. The suite was lovely, featuring an incredible full-size bath by the window, perfect for relaxing both body and soul, and the views from our room were fantastic. 
However, daily weddings disrupted the peaceful atmosphere, with guests around the pool and main grass area making it uncomfortable to be in swimwear. 
Construction work next to the hotel started at 8 a.m. and was noisy throughout the day, becoming more of a nuisance over time. 
On the bright side, the beach and beach bar were the highlights, offering excellent service, delicious food and drinks—don’t miss trying the coconuts, they were amazing.

It was so delicious, comfortable, and happy, beyond my expectations!
Thank you so much to Christina for her service!!
We had a truly wonderful honeymoon!!

So delicious! She makes us a very happy honeymoon!! ^_^
Thanks so much!!
Love everything about this place, the views are breathtaking taking. The service is out of this world! Mbak Yuni is taking a great care of us and give us complimentary during our honeymoon trips

Stayed two nights at the Kempinski Hotel Budapest during a business trip, mainly because of its very central location. The location is indeed excellent and convenient for short walking distances in the city center. Overall, the hotel leaves a decent first impression.

However, several aspects do not meet the expectations of a 5-star Kempinski hotel. The small shampoo bottles in the shower feel outdated and more typical of a 3-star property. The room itself was clean, but I had booked a room with a view and unfortunately received a room facing a wall instead of Fashion Street.

Breakfast was disappointing. While the food quality was acceptable, it was nothing special. The service was very poor—basic processes like serving coffee were inefficient and disorganized. This was my second stay; the first one was already average, but this time the service quality declined further.

Considering a nightly rate of EUR 250–350, there are significantly better 5-star hotel options in Budapest. Staff response to feedback was limited to a polite apology, without any solution or gesture of goodwill. The hotel also shows signs of age and a lack of attention to detail.

Due to the overall service level and value for money, I will not return to this hotel and will choose alternative 5-star properties in Budapest in the future.

Thank you for having us. We had a wonderful stay at 22 Summits. Our room was stylish, comfortable, and offered a stunning view of the Matterhorn. The staff were attentive and the breakfast selection was excellent. The location is ideal: set just far enough from the busy center to feel peaceful, yet still within easy walking distance of everything.

We cannot thank Sinta Paramita and Bu Dhanny enough for the incredible support they gave us throughout our wedding journey at The Apurva Kempinski Bali back in 30th November 2025. From the very beginning, they made us feel truly cared for, handling every detail with such kindness, patience, and professionalism.

Sinta was always attentive and reassuring, making the planning process feel smooth and stress-free. Her warm communication and willingness to help with every little request meant so much to us.

Bu Dhanny brought such grace and expertise on the wedding day itself. She ensured everything flowed beautifully, allowing us to simply be present and enjoy each moment.

Thanks to both of them, our wedding became even more magical than we imagined. We are deeply grateful for their dedication, warmth, and heartfelt support. Highly recommended for anyone planning their special day at this stunning venue.

Absolutely amazing! We had the fortune of having the same driver (Surya) for airport pick ups and drop offs and he was very helpful. Super friendly staff at Kempenski and beautiful food. Will be definitely be back again.
One of best hotels we ever stayed. I could not find any faults.

Overall, the hotel is high standard and the room was excellent. Breakfast was fabulous and the service was good. However, a few things stood out to me as disappointing for an hotel that is supposed to be the best in the city.

1. Upon arrival, I would have expected at least a welcome drink-water, tea, champagne, something to greet guests. It was surprising to see so many staff standing at the entrance while we waited in line to check in, with no one offering any refreshment. This fell below my expectations for Kempinski.

2. The spa-which we were very excited about after all the hype surrounding it as the best in Budapest-was, in one word, disappointing. The pool water wasn’t warm-it was cold! The pool is all one depth with no in-water seating areas. It’s poorly maintained, with a dangerously slippery floor. There’s only one ladder in and out-unsafe and impractical. No one offered us water at the entrance, we had to ask. That should be a given in any spa.

3. Why is there no small light in the bathroom? If someone gets up at night, they have to switch on a blinding light, which wakes up their partner and is harsh on the eyes after sleep.

4. The lobby restrooms were consistently dirty. It seemed like many people from the street were using them, and the hotel staff couldn't keep up with maintenance.

5. There’s a restaurant that stays empty for half the day, and the outdoor bar is often deserted. Why not offer a happy hour for hotel guests? Make them feel like they are prioritized in the hotel’s facilities.

6. Three nights with no turndown service. I expected to return to the room each evening to find it refreshed.

7. A small touch, like leaving a chocolate with a mid-stay note “We hope you’re enjoying your stay, here’s a sweet treat from us” would have made a huge difference.

8. I won’t get into the deeper details of LHW (Leading Hotels of the World) standards, like  knocking the door. It happened to us twice that HK entered the room without knocking first.
A basic courtesy that was surprisingly overlooked. you could certainly start with these points to improve from good to great.

I stayed at the hotel in early December. Budapest is magnificent, it leaves a lasting impression. Seeing Budapest from the Kempinski Hotel is the best thing ever. Thanks to Alexandra and Anastasia for their friendly attitude towards guests. I've recommended the Kempinski to my friends and will definitely be back myself.

We had dinner in the restaurant!!
Very cozy !
The stuff was very helpful and food delicious

I stayed in a two-bedroom villa. The rooms were beautiful, and the service was excellent. The hotel provided everything, including mosquito repellent for the room and for body application.
The entire staff was friendly, responsive, and very helpful.
If I have the chance again, I would definitely choose the Apurva Kempinski Hotel.
I was assisted by staff members Aryati and Ecik.
Thank you.

Breakfast is not recommended and the restaurant és is not a highlight! Decorated it is very tasteful and clean, but the staff is overwhelmed and chaos moderately confused.  Also sad that in Hungary the staff, at the reception, do not even speak Hungarian!! The rooms were too cold tempered, the water in the indoor pool as well!

It's impressive to stay here and not want to go anywhere.
Enjoy the complete facilities
The carelessness of care from the top staff to the staff resto pool and all the staff FL vendor all fun
Thanks to Tommy's staff at the pool area who worked hard to capture our family photos.
Best for all the food facilities and services
A day at the apurva kempinski bali won't be enough

Thank you Management of Apurva Kempinski absolutely amazing hotel with awesome courteous staff. It has been a fabulous experience starting from poolside reef club  to dinner to breakfast. 
Heart filled with gratitude for all staff members . It would be wrong to name few. All are superb. Special mention to Pushpa on check In time.
Amazing staff, food selection was of the highest standards, rooms and facilities weee incredible. Room selection was excellent, and very clean.

Excellent. 

I like to go here just because the area is quite large 😊

Lots of good photo spots, very effective experiences especially for kids

We are deeply grateful for the exceptional care during our 3-night stay. From the moment we arrived, the staff’s warmth made all the difference.

A special thank you to Atha, who greeted us with a big smile and genuine kindness, even during a busy morning—and kept that same energy all day. We also want to recognize Tommy, who went above and beyond to make the kids’ pool time so joyful and memorable.

The positive spirit was felt everywhere—front desk, restaurant, housekeeping, and more. Every smile made us feel truly at home, and every detail of our stay was handled with heart.

Thank you for making us feel not just welcomed, but truly loved. We hope this wonderful team continues to shine.

I had a great time , the service level is really unexpected, specially in Cliff lounge we had a great service from pandi and the crew members of the lounge , great views, great weather, great food and perfect omelettes
Kempinski Hotel Budapest is one of the best hotels, centrally located, large clean rooms with everything you need to enjoy. Wonderful room service, excellent breakfast.

For our last dinner of our honeymoon, we went to the Chinese restaurant BAIYUN. They greeted us warmly from the moment we arrived and continued to be kind and attentive until we finished our meal! I was truly blessed :)
And each dish was made with care, and most importantly, it was incredibly fresh, so I had no worries about what I was eating!
I definitely recommend visiting this place for a casual meal in addition to staying at the Kempinski!
And the staff were so approachable and polite, which was a real plus! They were the best!👍👍👍👍

Amazing time. Hotel was great. Zermatt was great. Amazing area , good food , safe , great staff , fun activities , easy , and clean. You should definitely go to Zermatt and stay in the 22 summit. I can't say enough great things about this hotel and staff I'll be coming back with my son when he gets older! Also delicious free breakfast.
I thought the service was only good for foreigners, but it turns out they are also friendly with Indonesians... nice, huh?

We spent 7 nights there. It was the most unpleasant and disagreeable stay we've ever had in Bali (we've been there over 25 times in various hotels).

The staff's friendliness was forced. They often walked past guests without even looking at them.

The breakfast room was very noisy. An employee who should have known us since the first day (she was always going around announcing the buffet's opening hours) asked me on the last day if this was our first time in the breakfast room!

We had problems with the safe twice in one day (in the morning it wouldn't close, and it took an hour for the technician to arrive, during which time we couldn't leave the room with the safe open. In the evening it wouldn't open and it beeped continuously). The safe was replaced after an unsuccessful repair attempt. As an apology, we received a card from the lady in red.

On the second-to-last day, there was a wedding reception. They started setting up the tent by the pool in the morning (loud and very disruptive), with music rehearsals followed by live music until 10 pm. We had room 6138 near the pool and the marquee.

It wasn't relaxing at all. The room wasn't well insulated; you could hear conversations and children screaming in the hallway very early in the morning.

On the day of departure, I almost stepped on a rather long queue on my way to reception. The employee who happened to be nearby and saw the queue just said "Snake" and kept walking.

At check-out, the lady didn't even bother to ask how our stay had been. When I mentioned that I had almost stepped on a queue, her response was that it's normal to have queues in a tropical country.

I also complained about the noise from the wedding celebration and pointed out that the hotel should have informed us about the noise at the time of booking or check-in. Her response was that if we had complained, they would have given us a different room.

On the positive side, the Bai Yun restaurant had very good quality food and friendly staff. But you can visit Bai Yun without being a guest at the Kempinski.

Heavy smell of humidity (mold) in the room. Strange management behavior after my friend had an accident because of reckless safety measures from the hotels side... Doesn't feel like a high level, 5 star hotel... Pity.

Hands down the best hotel that I've been. The photos will never do justice to the place.

If you have the budget, don't hesitate but stay at least 2-3 days to really appreciate the place.

I will definitely comeback with my family once I have the budget haha
Such a hidden gem in the heart of Budapest for a decent price.

A truly memorable experience
A beautiful resort staffed by beautiful people!!

It was a truly meaningful time. The staff were friendly, and the cocktails were delicious.
The memories I've made here will be ones I'll remember for the rest of my life.
You absolutely must visit this place.
Juli, mada, deva, cuditya, and meagel are very kind.
This place is so happy to me. Thank you for your service.
Very nice hotel, and the staff is wonderful.
It’s just perfect.
Top 👌
Very good place

A beautiful resort staffed by beautiful people 
Amenities and services were first class
Would definitely stay there again

Stunning Resort & Exceptional Staff, With a Few Dining Letdowns

Our stay at The Apurva Kempinski Bali was absolutely beautiful. The resort is breathtaking, and our villa was everything we dreamed of for our anniversary trip. The private pool, the large soaking tub, and the outdoor shower created such a luxurious and relaxing experience. The staff was truly outstanding — warm, attentive, and always willing to help. They really made our stay memorable.

Our only disappointment came from the dining experiences. We visited two restaurants on the property and unfortunately both fell short. At the beachside restaurant, several of the items we wanted from the menu were unavailable. At the rooftop restaurant, we were excited to try the pineapple shisha — especially since it was displayed in the lobby video — but after sitting down and ordering, we were told it wasn’t available. This was discouraging, especially since we were celebrating our anniversary and looking forward to that specific moment.

We also considered dining at the underwater restaurant, but before booking we asked if we could take a quick peek to get a sense of the atmosphere — especially after our earlier restaurant disappointments. We were told we couldn’t enter without a reservation, so we skipped it altogether. It would have been nice to at least view the space to decide if it was worth the experience.

Aside from the restaurant issues, everything about the resort itself was exceptional. The villa, the setting, the amenities, and especially the service made our stay wonderful. With improvements to dining availability and flexibility, this resort would easily be a perfect 5-star experience.

There's nothing to do nearby, so taking a taxi costs 20,000 won. The air conditioning is noisy and not soundproof, but the service is good. The food is delicious, the accommodations are huge, and everyone is friendly. If you come at the regular price, breakfast is included, but since I got everything for 600,000 won for 2 nights including breakfast, I have no regrets. But if it's the regular price, why bother?

A special thanks to dwi, Mada, Dwika, Miguel and Deva from the Cliff Lounge for making our night.

It was truly one of the best experience.
There is only one word: Perfecto!!

This was our second time staying at Kempinski Bali (May 2024) but unfortunately the service this time was not as smooth as before. The check-in process was quite tough, especially when it came to the room request. We asked for a high floor and a corner room, which we had ALREADY noted in advance. During check in , They told us the room wasn’t available, but the way it was communicated felt stiff and unfriendly.

The communication from the team was very poor — almost no sincere smile, and the attitude felt a bit snobbish. We understand that The Apurva is one of the high-end hotels in Nusa Dua, but that should be even more reason for them to treat guests with warmth and professionalism.

After a long discussion, and only when we pushed harder, they finally gave us the room we needed — but with an extra charge. The whole process felt tiring and unnecessary.

Sadly, this was a bad experience for us.
With service like this. It's a NO for going back.

We recently stayed at Kempinski Bali with our family, and it was truly an unforgettable experience. Mr. Wasudewa and Mr. Restu assisted us with genuine warmth and full dedication throughout our stay, making our family holiday exceptionally comfortable and memorable.

We also appreciated the valuable information shared by Mr. Dewa regarding the hotel’s facilities, which helped us enjoy our time even more.

Thank you to the wonderful team at Kempinski Bali. Because of their outstanding service, we look forward to returning again very soon.

Heaven on earth. Highly recommend this hotel. The food, staff and location, services and accomodation is amazing.

Thank you Meri and Widi at Aruyva spa for a wonderful 90 minute deep tissue massage. It was our first massage here and it was amazing.

TAMA is genuinely the best person I’ve ever met, he is always so happy, great service always going above and beyond at the pool bar. Made my experience so incredible would definitely come back if he was there.

We had a lovely dinner at the Japanese restaurant at The Apurva Kempinski Bali. The atmosphere was calm and elegant, and it ended up being the perfect place to celebrate my birthday.

The food was beautifully presented and very fresh, especially the sushi and sashimi. Everything felt carefully prepared without being overdone.

A special thank you to Jacqui Lin, who really made the evening feel personal. She was warm, attentive, and noticed it was my birthday. She went out of her way to spoil me a little, which was such a thoughtful touch and made the night feel extra special.

It was a really enjoyable experience, and I appreciated the kindness and care shown throughout the evening.

The location is excellent, but everyone who has ever stayed here has already mentioned that, so I won’t repeat it. Let’s move on to the real highlight — the attraction called “5-star premium you definitely didn’t expect.”

Let’s start with the bathroom. The hotel decided to surprise me with a level of minimalism so intense that there was no shampoo or conditioner. Apparently, they believe true luxury is the ability to wash your hair using the power of thought. Normally, these essentials come standard, but here “standard” seems to be a creative concept. I had to go to reception and gently explain that having clean hair is not a whim — it’s a basic human need.

The TV also follows its own philosophy. It only shows news and sports — not a single movie, no music, absolutely no entertainment. It feels like they launched a “digital detox” program and just forgot to tell the guests. Though, to be fair, they did offer us a bottle of champagne — perhaps to help us cope with the detox through a different kind of suffering.

Now for the restaurant. We ordered goulash. They brought it… warm. Not hot, not cold — specifically “lukewarm, but enjoy.” As for the bread — “not available right now, but on the way.” Apparently, the bread was traveling from another city, because it arrived 10 minutes later, exactly when the goulash had already become a memory. Hunger may be impatient, but not that impatient.

The service also strives to impress. It feels less like a premium hotel and more like a roadside diner “between nowhere and further away.” The waitress didn’t finish taking our order — she simply zoomed off to another table as if they were giving out awards at the back of the hall. Service? Customer? Probably words from a future that hasn’t yet reached this place.

The evening walk through the corridor is a special genre. Every night, the hallway is decorated with dirty dishes and bags of trash. The path from the elevator to the room turns into an exhibition of modern art called “What the Guests Left Out.” Perhaps it’s an experimental “open space concept,” but I sincerely wish they would close it.

Toward the end of our stay, a small ray of light appeared: reception extended our stay for an extra night at the same price, keeping all discounts — for that, truly, thank you. But during checkout, the manager seemed to be in a terrible mood, and since my English isn’t perfect, he chose to switch into aggression mode instead of communication. Fortunately, everything ended with a complimentary airport transfer — probably to make sure we left quickly without further discussions.

Final verdict?
If you enjoy roller-coaster service, unexpected surprises, and the genre “absurdist comedy,” this hotel is perfect for you.
If your vacation is not a gamble — better skip it. This place can surprise you, but not necessarily in a good way.
Great time and very beautiful
favourite place I like to visit 🥰
The place, location, amenities, services and staff all are excellent, can’t ask for more. We spend 2 nights here and everything exceeds our expectations
Calm and majestic buildings, gardens, etc. are wonderful. The facilities are excellent. The staff are generally kind and likable. It's best if you have daily maintenance on every corner.

Very central hotel, clean rooms. Very friendly staff and lovely spa area

We would like to express our deepest gratitude to your wonderful team for the amazing service we received during our stay. We were only there for 3 nights, but in that short time, we truly experienced the best that a hotel can offer.

We checked in early in the morning, and a staff member named Atha guided us to our room. He was incredibly friendly and professional , even though he was clearly working hard,sweating out , he still greeted us with genuine warmth and a big smile. Later that evening, we saw him again and were so surprised to see that he still had the same energy and kindness he showed in the morning. It’s not easy to show up like that all day long, and we really appreciated it.

Throughout our stay, we could feel that same positive energy from everyone ;from the concierge and front desk, to the lobby staff, restaurant team, gardeners, maintenance, and housekeeping. Every smile made the hotel feel alive. Every interaction made us feel welcomed. The food, the facilities, and all the activities added so much joy to our trip. On our last day, the kids’ pool was the perfect final treat. Tommy took such great care of the children ,playing with them , even though it might not technically be part of his job. Coming from a hospitality background ourselves, we truly recognize and appreciate the extra effort he put in. We didn’t just feel like guests ,we felt at home. As guests, we felt cared for.
As humans, we felt loved.
We sincerely hope the hotel continues supporting its staff so they can keep doing what they do best ; sharing their humanity, their warmth, and those beautiful smiles that make all the difference.

Thank you for making our stay unforgettable.

All the positive reviews we read are well presented. However, this hotel did not live up to our expectations. We came to relax and indulge in this  5 Star  location. Here is our singular take on the hotel. The staff are delightful which is a Bali given. They tried so hard but here is where it misses the mark for us. The lighting is too dark which compromises our vision. The beach facility is probably the best but there are no attendants for towels or beverages. 1. Design and Ambience
	•	Architecture: Mesmerising. Every corner looks intentional. Every surface, finish, and furnishing feels carefully chosen.
	•	Aesthetic Impact: Every photo looked beautiful without even trying. The place is visually stunning from every angle.
	•	Landscape & Walkability: I enjoyed the long walks. The combination of nature, architecture, and open spaces made the resort feel alive. Not ideal for those who dislike walking, but perfect for anyone who enjoys being outdoors.

2. Facilities
	•	Pool: Enormous. Never boring. Easily one of the highlights.
	•	Kids Club: Surprisingly impressive. Clean, well run, and engaging. My kids loved it and were disappointed whenever they had to leave.
	•	Playground: Basic but fun. Conveniently placed right opposite the Kids Club. The rain limited our time there, but the kids preferred swimming anyway.

3. Villa & Room Experience
	•	Two-Bedroom Villa: One of the pricier options. Spacious and comfortable.
	•	Cleanliness: This was the single weak point. Dusty corners and surfaces made the room feel below the standard expected of a seven-star resort. A little more attention to housekeeping would have made a big difference.

4. Food & Dining
	•	Overall Quality: Food was great. Pricey, but expected for a resort of this level.
	•	Reef Beach Club: Lovely spot for dinner with a great atmosphere.
	•	Coral Restaurant: Memorable, once-in-a-lifetime experience. Beautiful, unique, but personally a bit too expensive for a repeat visit.

5. Service
	•	Staff: Friendly, respectful, and consistently accommodating.
	•	Atmosphere: Warm smiles, quick assistance, and a generally pleasant experience throughout the stay.

6. Final Thoughts
	•	What I Loved Most: The architecture, the landscape, the pool, the kids facilities, the attentive service.
	•	What Could Be Improved: Room cleanliness. Everything else lived up to the reputation.
	•	Overall: A visually stunning and thoughtfully designed resort that delivers a memorable stay. I would return, but I’d hope for better housekeeping next time.

I just returned from the most wonderful 6 days here. Everything about this resort is five star. In particular, I want to say how absolutely fantastic every member of the team is, and thank them for making my stay a great experience from start to end. Everyone I met is warm, friendly and keen to help you in any way, But in particular I would like to thank Kiki (who showed me all the features of my room when I first arrived), Putra and Junaidi (who did the most wonderful job servicing my room each day, and always checking to see if there was anything I needed), Indri (in the day spa, who gave me the best massage I have ever had), and Devi (who looked after me several times at the fantastic Izakaya restaurant). I stayed in a Cliff room with Club access and the breakfasts were absolutely amazing. I tried many of the a la carte options, all were delicious. I particularly liked the fact that the al a carte servings were smaller, so you could try a couple at each breakfast. And then I headed to the buffet for more amazing options - wonderful fresh fruit, yoghurt, incredible bread and pastries, sushi, dumplings, salads - and so many other options. The beach here is spotlessly clean, as the resort is a bit further away from the main Nusa Dua city area (which I enjoyed because it is much quieter). I went to Bali Collection once, and that is only a 10 minute taxi ride away. The pools were all spotless and the gardens gorgeous. I loved this resort so much, I have already booked to go back again next year.  One suggestion I would like to make is that the Cliff Junior Suites each have pools, but for older guests it would be helpful to have a pool handrail to make getting in and out safer and easier.
Thank you to everyone at Apurva Kempinski for a wonderful experience.

Went to Apurva for a one-night stay with my boyfriend because we were curious to try two of their restaurants — Bai Yun and Oku. Both restaurants were amazing! The service was superb, and Oku even exceeded my expectations. We had such a great time. The next morning, breakfast was also really nice — great service, good food, and plenty of options.

However, we were disappointed with the room.
- The bathtub was broken and couldn’t drain. We first reported it around 4 PM and asked the Front Office to fix it while we were out at 5 PM. When we returned at 7 PM, nothing had been done. We called again and requested it to be fixed while we were having dinner at 8 PM. It was finally drained, but once we used the bathtub again, it got stuck and wouldn’t drain again. I think they have an underlying issues here.
- The pool also wasn’t great, which was surprising — I’m not sure how no one else seems to mention this. It definitely didn’t feel like the standard of a 5-star hotel.
- There was a cockroach on the bedroom wall. We didn’t complain since it wasn’t a big deal to us, but their pest maintenance should really be more thorough. We were on the 5th floor and never opened the balcony, so it was quite unusual to see one inside the room.

I genuinely hope these issues can be fixed, because the service and restaurants were fantastic — but the room experience really needs improvement. But will definitely give it another try next time!

So I needed boiled betel leaves and other herbs for herbal medicine, and the staff went out of their way to find betel leaves, even though they didn't have any. The best part was that there wasn't any medicine, and the service was really great. 🥹🥹 I'll be back!

This place was out of this world! We stayed in a Grand Deluxe room and it did not disappoint. It was clean, extremely comfortable & had the highest standard of finishings. We had a morning housekeeping change over and an evening 7turn 7down daily, which was an absolute treat. We cannot get over how amazing all the staff are, from the moment you arrive at the lobby to walking past staff in the hallways. Everyone is kind, courteous, helpful and hospitable. No matter who you are, staff will always acknowledge you and just seem so incredibly happy and grateful for you being here.

Santika and Tedy were our housekeepers and they ensured our rooms stayed as clean & comfortable as possible. Thank you so much for your care! A special mention to Tommie and the pool team for keeping the kids safe and entertained while on the slides.

We highly reccomend staying here, the facilities are incredible and we are truly sad our stay is coming to an end.
This was the perfect honeymoon destination. The hospitality, service, and food were all perfect. The staff were exceptionally friendly, and I'd love to visit again.
